title: 7.-Contract: Patagonia Health Electronic Health Records System Implementation for Berkeley High School Health Center

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager or designee to execute a contract and
any amendments with Patagonia Health for implementation of an Electronic Health
Record (EHR) system for the Berkeley High School Health Center in an amount not to
exceed $260,000 for the period of November 1, 2025 through June 30, 2030.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Through a competitive Request for Proposal (RFP) process, staff have selected
Patagonia Health to provide and implement their EHR system at Berkeley High School
Health Center. The City conducted an RFP process and received four proposals.
Patagonia Health was selected as the highest scoring bidder and submitted a quote
with final pricing. The contract will have a total not to exceed amount of $260,000.
Funding is available in the FY 2026 Adopted Budget in Public Health Realignment
(Fund 156).
The current EHR system being used at the BHSHC is an outdated version of the
software, one that is unsupported by the vendor, and costs approximately $190,000
annually to maintain. The implementation of this new EHR system is estimated to be
$100,000 with an average of $40,000 annually for user licenses and maintenance of the
system for the subsequent four years. This upgrade would substantially improve the
EHR functionality and reduce ongoing maintenance and licensing costs of the EHR by
$150,000 annually.
sections.current_situation:
The Berkeley High School Health Center offers free and confidential reproductive
sexual health, mental health, and first aid services for over 3,000 students that are 12-
20 years old at Berkeley High School and Berkeley Technology Academy.
The current EHR system was implemented approximately 20 years ago. The City has
not updated the software for the last 10 years. The current system requires integration

with several other software systems and is currently operating on an outdated version
that is unsupported by the vendor. The upgrade of the current EHR system would
require a substantial financial investment which prompted an RFP process to determine
a solution that meets the operational needs of the clinic that was financially competitive.
The new EHR system would integrate appointment scheduling, intake, medical record
documentation, and billing seamlessly. This new system would decrease the need to
integrate a number of technological solutions to support the operations and allow for
maximization of timely revenue from eligible services.
sections.background:
The City released an RFP under Specification No. 25-11692-C and convened a panel of
stakeholders, who selected Patagonia Health as the most responsive and responsible
bidder for this contract.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identifiable environmental effects, climate impacts, or sustainability
opportunities associated with the subject of this report.
sections.rationale:
Patagonia Health has been in business since 2009 and is a well-respected EHR vendor
with demonstrated subject matter expertise on building systems to support public health
and clinic operations. Their proposal in response to RFP Specification No. 25-11692-C
includes high quality services that align with the intended goals of maximizing clinic
operations and billing procedures.
sections.alternatives:
Staff did not identify an alternative action consistent with the City’s goals of advancing
efficient and sustainable City government operations. The Council may choose not to
approve the contract; however, staff would not adequately be able to document, track,
and report on critical student health data.
CONTACT PERSON
Tiffany Simpson, Senior Health Services Program Specialist, HHCS, (510) 644-6308

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: PATAGONIA HEALTH ELECTRONIC HEALTH RECORDS SYSTEM
IMPLEMENTATION
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley Department of Health, Housing & Community Services
(HHCS), is committed to providing comprehensive public health services to Medi-Cal
eligible clients, and
WHEREAS, HHCS seeks to eliminate health and educational inequities; and
WHEREAS, PATAGONIA HEALTH was selected by the City’s RFP process which
includes bidding from vendors, draft proposals, and pricing; and
WHEREAS, Funding is available in the FY2026 adopted budget in Public Health
Realignment (Fund 156)
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager or designee is authorized to execute a contract and any amendments with
Patagonia Health for implementation of an Electronic Health Record system for the
Berkeley High School Health Center in an amount not to exceed $260,000 for the period
of November 1, 2025 through June 30, 2030
